reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 2 cfs @ Fort Collins , 1 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir The warmer, spring-like weather this week has started pushing snowmelt down the canyon. Flows are on the rise, and the water is a bit off-color. That said, fishing has still been great! Looks like an early, light terrestrial bite is starting to show up. Consider running a dry-dropper rig in the afternoons, as there are plenty of ants and beetles moving around. With temps climbing on the Poudre, bug diversity is improving. Larger Stonefly and caddis imitations have been finding success. With the off-color water, attractor patterns and brighter flies have been producing as well. Spring is definitely starting to show its face around here, and we’re getting more guide trips out on the Poudre. Give us a call to get the lowdown! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
